title: Food & Beverage Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Food & Beverage Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a food business? Cost breakdowns for restaurants, food trucks, coffee shops, bakeries, bars, and more.
content:
Food & Beverage Business Startup Costs

Food and beverage businesses range from a $5,000 coffee cart to a $750,000 full-service restaurant. They share one thing in common: razor-thin margins that punish sloppy financial planning. Food costs, labor costs, and permitting complexity make this the most demanding category for first-time entrepreneurs - and also one of the most rewarding when you get it right.

Every guide below includes a full cost breakdown, hidden expenses, realistic breakeven timelines, and the cost-reduction strategies that separate survivors from the 60% of food businesses that close within a year.

Cost Guides

Restaurant - $175,000-$750,000

The full breakdown: buildout, kitchen equipment, liquor licensing, pre-opening labor, and the three-month cash reserve most owners don't budget for.

Food Truck - $28,000-$114,000

Three paths to getting on the road - used truck DIY, pre-built conversion, and custom build. Plus the commissary kitchen requirement most first-timers miss.

Coffee Shop - $25,000-$300,000

From kiosk to full cafe. Espresso machine selection, buildout costs, and the afternoon revenue gap nobody warns you about.

Bakery - Coming Soon

Home kitchen to commercial bakery. Equipment, permitting, and the wholesale vs. retail revenue model decision.

Bar - Coming Soon

Liquor licensing, buildout, inventory, and the economics of a business that makes most of its money between 9 PM and 2 AM.

Catering Business - Coming Soon

Lower overhead than a restaurant with higher per-event margins. Commissary kitchens, equipment, and the sales cycle.

Brewery - Coming Soon

Brewing equipment, TTB licensing, taproom buildout, and distribution economics. From a $100,000 nanobrewery to a $2M+ production facility.

Juice Bar - Coming Soon

Commercial juicers, cold-press equipment, produce sourcing, and the perishability challenge that drives your margins.

title: Service Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Service Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a service business? Cost breakdowns for cleaning, landscaping, pressure washing, painting, and more.
content:
Service Business Startup Costs

Service businesses are the lowest-barrier entry point into entrepreneurship. Many can be started for under $5,000 with equipment you already own and skills you can learn in weeks. The trade-off: you're selling your time, which means your income is capped by the number of hours you can work - until you hire employees and learn to run a business instead of being the business.

These guides cover the full cost picture from solo operator to scaled operation, including the equipment, insurance, and marketing needed to get your first 20 clients.

Cost Guides

Cleaning Business - $1,500-$15,000

One of the cheapest businesses to start that generates real income. Solo residential cleaners can net $80,000-$110,000/year.

Landscaping Business - $5,000-$50,000

Mow-and-go lawn care versus full-service landscaping - two different businesses at two different price points. Seasonal revenue planning included.

Pressure Washing Business - $3,000-$20,000

One of the best risk-to-reward ratios in small business. 85-95% gross margins per job with a $3,000 startup investment.

Painting Business - Coming Soon

Equipment, insurance, lead certification, and the per-project pricing model that drives revenue.

Plumbing Business - Coming Soon

Licensing requirements, tool costs, van setup, and the apprenticeship path to master plumber.

HVAC Business - Coming Soon

Equipment, EPA certification, licensing, and the recurring revenue model that makes HVAC one of the most profitable trades.

Window Cleaning Business - Coming Soon

Low startup costs, year-round demand, and the commercial contracts that build recurring revenue.

title: Health & Fitness Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Health & Fitness Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a gym, salon, or fitness studio? Cost breakdowns for gyms, hair salons, spas, yoga studios, and more.
content:
Health & Fitness Business Startup Costs

Health, fitness, and beauty businesses combine high startup costs with recurring revenue models - memberships, regular appointments, and repeat visits that create predictable income once you're established. The challenge is surviving long enough to fill your chairs, classes, or membership roster. Equipment costs are significant, buildout requirements are specific, and licensing adds complexity.

Cost Guides

Gym / Fitness Studio - $50,000-$500,000

Boutique studio versus full-service gym. Equipment costs, pre-sale strategies, and the membership math that determines survival.

Hair Salon - $60,000-$250,000

Buildout, stations, product inventory, and the commission vs. booth rental decision that shapes your entire business model.

Barbershop - Coming Soon

Lower startup costs than a full salon, higher client turnover, and the chair rental economics.

Yoga Studio - Coming Soon

Minimal equipment, maximum ambiance. Space design, instructor costs, and membership pricing models.

Personal Training Studio - Coming Soon

Small space, high-value clients, and per-session economics that outperform gym memberships.

Day Spa - Coming Soon

Treatment rooms, esthetics licensing, product inventory, and the average ticket that justifies premium buildout.

Nail Salon - Coming Soon

Station setup, ventilation requirements, licensing, and the volume-based revenue model.

title: Pet Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Pet Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a pet business? Cost breakdowns for dog grooming, pet sitting, dog daycare, training, and more.
content:
Pet Business Startup Costs

The pet industry generates over $150 billion annually in the US, and it's one of the most recession-resistant sectors in the economy - people cut their own spending before they cut spending on their pets. Pet businesses range from near-zero startup costs (dog walking) to six figures (dog daycare facilities), with strong demand and loyal client bases across all segments.

Cost Guides

Dog Grooming Business - $5,000-$100,000

Home setup, mobile van, or salon - three models with very different economics. Plus the per-dog math that drives profitability.

Dog Daycare - Coming Soon

Facility requirements, insurance, staffing ratios, and the recurring daily revenue that makes this model work.

Pet Sitting Business - Coming Soon

Near-zero startup costs with the flexibility to scale from side hustle to full-time operation.

Dog Training Business - Coming Soon

Certification paths, equipment needs, and per-session pricing that generates $75-$150/hour.

Pet Store - Coming Soon

Inventory, retail lease, specialty product sourcing, and the grooming/service add-ons that drive margins.

title: Automotive Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Automotive Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start an automotive business? Cost breakdowns for trucking, auto repair, car washes, detailing, and more.
content:
Automotive Business Startup Costs

Automotive businesses are equipment-intensive and regulation-heavy, but they serve markets with consistent demand - people always need their vehicles serviced, their goods shipped, and their cars cleaned. These businesses reward operators who understand their cost per unit (per mile, per vehicle, per wash) and manage their equipment investment carefully.

Cost Guides

Trucking Company - $15,000-$200,000

Owner-operator economics, MC authority, insurance shock for new carriers, and the cash flow trap that kills trucking startups.

Auto Repair Shop - Coming Soon

Equipment, facility, ASE certification, and the diagnostic technology investment that keeps you competitive.

Car Wash - Coming Soon

Self-serve, automatic, and hand wash models. Equipment costs, water usage, and the membership model revolutionizing the industry.

Mobile Detailing Business - Coming Soon

Low startup costs with premium pricing. Equipment, supplies, and the per-vehicle economics.

Tow Truck Business - Coming Soon

Truck costs, dispatch relationships, police rotation lists, and the per-tow revenue model.

title: Education & Childcare Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Education & Childcare Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a daycare or education business? Cost breakdowns for daycares, preschools, tutoring, and after-school programs.
content:
Education & Childcare Business Startup Costs

Education and childcare businesses are the most heavily regulated category on this site. Licensing requirements, staff-to-child ratios, facility standards, and background check mandates create a higher barrier to entry than most industries - but they also create a significant competitive moat once you're established. Demand consistently exceeds supply in most markets.

Cost Guides

Daycare - $10,000-$250,000

Home daycare versus commercial center - two different businesses with very different licensing, costs, and economics. CACFP food program, grants, and staffing ratios explained.

Preschool - Coming Soon

Curriculum requirements, facility standards, teacher qualifications, and the half-day model that changes the economics.

Tutoring Business - Coming Soon

Low startup costs, high per-hour rates, and the online vs. in-person model decision.

After-School Program - Coming Soon

Shared facility model, shorter operating hours, and the school-age ratios that reduce staffing costs.

title: Professional Services Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Professional Services Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a professional services business? Cost breakdowns for accounting firms, law firms, consulting, and more.
content:
Professional Services Business Startup Costs

Professional services businesses have the highest profit margins and lowest startup costs of any category - your primary "equipment" is your expertise. The trade-off: most require professional licensing or credentials, and your revenue is directly tied to your billable hours until you hire associates. These are knowledge businesses that scale on reputation and referrals.

Cost Guides

Accounting Firm - Coming Soon

CPA licensing, software, E&O insurance, and the tax season revenue concentration problem.

Law Firm - Coming Soon

Bar admission, malpractice insurance, office setup, and the client acquisition costs by practice area.

Consulting Business - Coming Soon

Near-zero startup costs for solo consultants. Positioning, pricing, and the project vs. retainer revenue model.

Bookkeeping Business - Coming Soon

Lower barrier than accounting - no CPA required. Software costs, certification, and the recurring monthly revenue model.

Real Estate Agency - Coming Soon

Licensing, brokerage fees, MLS access, marketing, and the commission structure that determines your income.

title: Creative & Media Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Creative & Media Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a creative business? Cost breakdowns for photography, graphic design, videography, and content creation.
content:
Creative & Media Business Startup Costs

Creative businesses trade on skill and portfolio rather than physical infrastructure. Startup costs are moderate - primarily equipment and software - but building a client base takes time and your portfolio is your most important marketing asset. These businesses reward specialists over generalists.

Cost Guides

Photography Business - Coming Soon

Camera gear, lighting equipment, software, insurance, and the pricing model by specialty (wedding, portrait, commercial).

Graphic Design Business - Coming Soon

Software subscriptions, hardware, portfolio building, and the freelance vs. agency model.

Videography Business - Coming Soon

Camera and audio equipment, editing software, and the per-project pricing that drives this market.

Content Creation Business - Coming Soon

Equipment, platform strategy, monetization models, and the timeline from launch to sustainable income.

title: Construction & Trades Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Construction & Trades Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a construction or trades business? Cost breakdowns for electrical, plumbing, general contracting, and more.
content:
Construction & Trades Business Startup Costs

Trades businesses require licensing, insurance, and tools - but the demand is relentless and skilled tradespeople are in short supply nationwide. These are businesses where expertise commands premium pricing and referrals build themselves. The licensing path is longer than most industries (apprenticeship + journeyman + master), but once you're established, the work finds you.

Cost Guides

Electrical Business - Coming Soon

Licensing path, tool costs, van setup, and the commercial vs. residential revenue difference.

Plumbing Business - Coming Soon

Master plumber licensing, equipment, service van buildout, and emergency service pricing premiums.

General Contracting - Coming Soon

Contractor licensing by state, insurance, bonding requirements, and project management costs.

Roofing Business - Coming Soon

Equipment, safety gear, insurance, licensing, and the seasonal revenue pattern.

title: Online Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Online Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start an online business? Cost breakdowns for e-commerce, dropshipping, SaaS, freelancing, and digital businesses.
content:
Online Business Startup Costs

Online businesses have the lowest startup costs and the widest potential reach of any category. Many can be launched for under $1,000 from a laptop. The trade-off: low barriers to entry mean intense competition, and the marketing costs to stand out can dwarf your product costs. These businesses reward speed, testing, and iteration.

Cost Guides

E-commerce Store - Coming Soon

Platform costs (Shopify, WooCommerce), inventory, fulfillment, and the customer acquisition math that drives profitability.

Dropshipping Business - Coming Soon

Near-zero inventory costs but thin margins and customer service challenges. The real numbers behind the "passive income" marketing.

Freelance Writing Business - Coming Soon

Literally $0 to start if you own a laptop. Portfolio building, client acquisition, and the per-word vs. per-project pricing debate.

Print on Demand - Coming Soon

Design tools, platform fees, and the unit economics of selling custom products with no inventory.

title: Real Estate Business Startup Costs
seoTitle: Real Estate Business Startup Costs | Complete Guide
metaDescription: How much does it cost to start a real estate business? Cost breakdowns for agencies, property management, house flipping, and rental properties.
content:
Real Estate Business Startup Costs

Real estate businesses range from low-cost licensing-based models (becoming an agent) to capital-intensive investment models (house flipping, rental properties). The common thread: these businesses build long-term wealth through asset appreciation and recurring revenue, but they require patience, market knowledge, and - for investment models - significant capital or access to financing.

Cost Guides

Real Estate Agency - Coming Soon

Licensing, brokerage fees, MLS access, marketing, and the commission math that determines whether you make it past year one.

Property Management Company - Coming Soon

Software, insurance, licensing, and the per-door revenue model that builds recurring income.

House Flipping - Coming Soon

Acquisition costs, renovation budgets, holding costs, and the deal analysis framework that separates profitable flips from expensive mistakes.

Rental Property Business - Coming Soon

Down payments, financing, property management, and the cash flow analysis for your first investment property.
